successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveRequest, CreateLeaveRequestDto, and UpdateLeaveRequestStatusDto interfaces
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports ILeaveRepository interface and PgLeaveRequestRepository class
PgLeaveRequestRepository implements all repository methods using PostgreSQL pool with proper error handling
All repository methods use the exact SQL schema provided with correct column names and types
Repository tests in src/modules/leave/leave.repository.test.ts pass with Vitest
src/modules/leave/index.ts exports the repository and types

constraints: GP-001: All database operations use repository pattern
GP-006: Error handling - No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled
Repository pattern must be implemented with ILeaveRepository interface
PostgreSQL schema must match the provided SQL exactly
TypeScript interfaces must match the provided specifications
Dependency direction: leave -> employee (via foreign key reference)
outOfScope: Leave request creation service with validation (Phase 2)
Any changes to employee, policy, balance, or notification modules
Reverse dependencies from other modules to leave module

ambiguities.description: The intent mentions importing LeaveType and LeaveStatus from src/shared/types/index.ts, but the provided interfaces use string literal unions ('ANNUAL' | 'SICK' | 'EMERGENCY') and ('PENDING' | 'APPROVED' | 'REJECTED' | 'CANCELLED'). Should we use the imported types or the string literals?
options: Use string literal unions as shown in interfaces

ambiguities.description: Should the repository implementation include the SQL schema creation/migration logic, or just assume the table exists?
